Sandy Giovese Vino Rosso, Italy

🍷 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack likes Sandy Giovese's chill-me cue and finds earth, dirt, strawberries, violets, and cherries. He says it tastes like real wine: lightweight, juicy, acidic, quietly complex, and such a knockout that he would aspire to make boxed wine like it; it ends as his favorite wine of the day.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with community average 9.2 across 3 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Mushrooms, Manchego & Parmesan, Beef, Venison, White Rice, Potato, Exotic Spices, Baking Spices.

Mack's Thoughts

Mack names Gobelsburg as an Austrian blend of Zweigelt, St. Laurent, and Pinot Noir. He finds zip, pop, beautiful balanced acidity, and the kind of real, intentional wine that begs to be drunk now anywhere, not just by the pool in summer.

Mack's Thoughts

Mack uses Schloss Lieser Riesling to explain Germany's fluted bottles and off-dry Riesling style. He gets classic petrol/kerosene, unripe pear, green apple, lemon zest, residual sugar balanced by strong acidity, and says the wine finishes dry enough to make Riesling and French fries a joyful pairing.

Schplink Gruner Veltliner 2019, Austria

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack is encouraged by Schplink Gruner Veltliner's Austrian origin and 2019 vintage, then gets red apple, spearmint, strong acidity, minerality, and a salty edge. He calls it textbook Gruner Veltliner: racy, mineral-driven, asparagus-friendly, high-quality for the boxed price, and evidence that boxed wines in the $30 range can level up.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$ value
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack starts the price ladder with the $18 California Cabernet, later naming it Sidekick. He notes that the back label only says California, then gets red berries, currant, red fruit, black fruit, and a hint of vanilla likely from oak. He calls it pretty good, really great, and well made, while saying it lacks the nuanced place-specific details that appear as the wines get more expensive.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

For the white elephant gift swap, Mack wants a sub-$20 bottle that feels different enough to fight over. He chooses a sparkling red Lambrusco, notes the lower alcohol may signal sweetness, recognizes the importer, and says the bubbles, red color, and festive look would grab attention.
Taster
André Hueston Mack

Mack's Thoughts

Mack goes in hoping Low Country Red might change his mind about Muscadine, but says it is not the one. It smells like aromatic, herbaceous Amaro and tastes like Amaro without the high alcohol; as wine it is not palatable to him, though he can imagine it working in a cocktail.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Steady State Cabernet Sauvignon, Napa Valley, approximately $59

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack moves from generic California into the $59 Napa Valley Cabernet, later identifying it as Steady State. He explains that Napa fruit carries a price because of the region's microclimates and soils, then gets herbs with more specificity: rosemary, sage, lavender, cassis, and a little lead. He says these subtle nuances help explain the jump from the $18 bottle.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

No separate sommelier/critic note has been verified into this card yet, so no source-specific outside-note links are shown.

Mack places Masera in Gavi and uses the screwcap to talk about Stelvin closures. In the glass he gets white flowers, green apple, citrus, and a beautiful, delightful, light white-wine style that can move Pinot Grigio drinkers into more interesting coastal Italian whites.

Aromatic / other white · Video tasting

Sun Goddess Ramato Pinot Grigio, Mary J. Blige

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$ value
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack explains Sun Goddess as ramato Pinot Grigio, where skin contact gives the wine its copper-pink color and more texture. He gets perfume, flower bouquet, white flowers, peach, nectarine, unexpected cantaloupe, a kiss of backend sweetness, and decent acid. He calls it interesting, authentic-feeling, and great quality for $25, giving it a loudspeaker emoji because he wants people to know about it.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

No separate sommelier/critic note has been verified into this card yet, so no source-specific outside-note links are shown.

Mack's Thoughts

Mack finds Tsali Notch White Muscadine wild, foxy, fruity, sweet, textured, and lightly acidic. He sees a higher quality level than some other Muscadine wines, but the style still is not to his taste and he personally would not serve it, though sweet-wine drinkers might like it.

Tenuta delle Terre Nere Etna Rosso

🍷 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack places Tenuta delle Terre Nere Etna Rosso on the volcanic soils of Mount Etna and focuses on the mineral character that comes from that setting. He finds beautiful color, rich aromatics, hearty pepper, refined white pepper, bright currant and cherry fruit without sweetness, metallic minerality, and says the wine makes him want to drink more Etna Rosso.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack identifies Tenuta Le Calcinaie Chianti Colli Senesi as 100% Sangiovese and uses it to explain Chianti's dusty, less-fruit-led character. He gets raspberries, blackberry, leather, dust, a rough but correct Chianti-style finish, body, structure, and enough framework to read as very much Chianti across the board.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Mack's Thoughts

Mack gets warm baking spices and a little hair-salon sulfur from Pedernales Tempranillo, which checks out for the grape to him. Compared with Rioja, this Texas version is lusher, bigger, rounder, higher in alcohol, clearly American, and one he likes.

Thalvin / Alain Graillot Syrocco Syrah, Morocco

🍷 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack is intrigued by Morocco, the Syrah grape, and the producer partnership. In the glass he gets dark Syrah color, meat, savory herbs, rosemary, and everything he wants Syrah to be, raising it to an A.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

No separate sommelier/critic note has been verified into this card yet, so no source-specific outside-note links are shown.

👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack introduces The Chook as a fun South Australian sparkling Shiraz, a style many people do not realize exists. He gets blackberry, blueberry, cocoa nib, richness, roundness, and plenty of red-wine tannin, then uses the bottle to explain traditional-method sparkling wine and why the labor usually affects price.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Mack's Thoughts

Mack identifies The Dreaming Tree as a California Cabernet and gets boysenberries, blackberries, red currants, and a tiny bit of cedar without much vanilla or new-oak signature. He finds decent tannins and finish but wants more body, structure, roundness, richness, and texture from Cabernet. For $15 he is not mad at it and thinks many drinkers would be happy.

Syrah / Rhone · Retail tasting: Trader Joe's

Trader Joe's Grand Reserve GSM, Grenache / Syrah / Mourvedre

🍷 🔎 🛒?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack uses the Grand Reserve GSM to explain Grenache, Syrah, and Mourvedre as Rhone grapes, then gets iron, metallic savoriness, blood-like Mourvedre character, pepper, cranberry, currant, tannin, and high alcohol. He wanted more lush fruit and layers and says better Cotes du Rhone can be found for the same or less money.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 8.8 across 1 note and community average 8.6 across 32 ratings.

Delectable public context

He wanted more lush fruit and layers and says better Cotes du Rhone can be found for the same or less money.

🍷 🔎 🛒?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank

Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack gets pronounced ripe Paso fruit from Trader Joe's Grand Reserve Meritage: ripe plum, red currant, cocoa, vanilla, and cherry. He likes it because the glass delivers what the label advertises: rich, ripe, bountiful fruit and a clear Bordeaux-style blend promise.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Trader Joe's Grower's Reserve Red Wine Blend 2023

🍷 🔎 🛒?
Rank
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack finds violets, raspberry, vanilla, cinnamon, and a chemically induced quality in Trader Joe's Grower's Reserve Red Wine Blend. Despite its organic label, it tastes manipulated to him, with fingernail-polish-remover notes, and he says he would not drink it.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 8.4 across 3 notes and community average 8.4 across 242 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Lamb, Chicken, Turkey, White Rice, Pasta, Potato, Herbs, Mushrooms.

Trader Joe's Napa Valley Sauvignon Blanc, exact tier not exposed in transcript

🥂 🔎 🛒?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack says Trader Joe's Reserve Sauvignon Blanc smells faintly of cotton candy, grass, herbs, salt, and ocean air. On the palate it has great acidity, creamy texture, lemon, and lemon verbena, and he calls it spot on, solid, true to Napa Sauvignon Blanc, and a bargain at $9.99.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Mack gets bell pepper, green pepper, cranberry, cinnamon, black currant, ripe fruit, impact, flavor, and richness from Trader Joe's Petit Reserve Carmenere. It lacks a little acidity, but at $7.99 he calls it a fun pizza-and-movie bottle to explore without much financial risk.

Mack finds lemon-lime, lemon custard, cotton candy, peach, nectarine, high acidity, and a touch of residual sugar in Trader Joe's Willamette Pinot Gris. He calls it pretty good, great at $9.99, and a winner because the Willamette Valley designation already signals more quality.

Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$ value
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack says the Platinum Reserve Oakville Cabernet smells like high-quality Napa Cabernet: cedar, leather, black currant, red currant, bitter chocolate, and integrated tannin. It is polished, balanced, mature, and an incredible $17.99 value, though it falls short on the finish.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Mack's Thoughts

Mack gets Pinot funk, cherry, raspberry, and dried dirt from Trader Joe's Reserve Pinot Noir. The alcohol dominates so much that he calls it somewhat undrinkable, too hot, and short on fruit, recommending a hard chill just to tame the burn.

Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine

Mack's Thoughts

Mack places True Wine Connoisseurs Prosecco in Italy's cool-climate Prosecco region and finds grapefruit, fig, white peach, and a slight nutmeg note. He calls it refreshing, easy to drink, alive, complex enough to skip the mimosa treatment, and a good example of protected-place sparkling wine made affordably by tank method.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Tussock Jumper Merlot, USA / California

🍷 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack ranks the broad USA / California Merlot label last before tasting because it lacks producer and place specificity, but the wine smells better than expected. He finds it solid and real, though hot and not fully balanced, and upgrades it from a label D to a C-minus after tasting.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

No separate sommelier/critic note has been verified into this card yet, so no source-specific outside-note links are shown.

Pinot Noir · Video tasting

Underwood Pinot Noir 2019, Oregon

🍷 🔎 🛒?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

This lands as a holiday-table bottle, the kind you open when the meal and the people matter. Mack's captured note, "For a large Thanksgiving, Mack wants enough affordable wine for a crowd and chooses Oregon Pinot Noir. He likes the New World fruit crossed with Old World earthy character and says the $14 bottle overdelivers enough to buy by the mixed case.", points less to technical tasting notes and more to occasion. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Mack's Thoughts

Mack says I/G Winery Petite Sirah smells like Petite Sirah: plummy, peppery, dry meat rub, vanilla, and nutmeg. It is good quality and not overpowering, but at $65 he wants more oomph and tannin, and says the local fruit cost is not reflected enough in the glass.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore

🍾 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack explains that Valdobbiadene Prosecco Superiore means a higher-quality Prosecco made under protected-name rules, largely from Glera. He sees mousse and carbonation, gets peach and nectarine, calls it refined within Prosecco's own purpose, and says it is festive, fun, everyday luxury, and a strong value at the party-starting job.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Mack's Thoughts

Mack describes Snow Farm Snow Trip as cloudy, unfined, unfiltered natural or low-intervention wine that looks like cider or sour beer. It smells like spiced applesauce and tomato juice, tastes bland and watered down, but he sees the style as a welcome bridge for sour-beer drinkers.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Veuve Clicquot 1973 Champagne

🍾 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$$ premium
Research bucket
Collector / benchmark bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack worries the 1973 Veuve is slightly corked, with wet cardboard under the aromas, pear juice from a can, butterscotch kombucha, and old white wine past its prime. He treats it as educational, showing caramelization and age after the wine's best life has passed.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Veuve Clicquot 1982 Champagne

🍾 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$$ premium
Research bucket
Collector / benchmark bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack says the 1982 Veuve smells like caramel chew and an old closet in the best way, with cocoa nibs or chocolate and only a faint prickling from the bubbles. Primary fruit has faded far back into cooked-down apple and pear, making it drink more like aged wine than Champagne.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 9.0 across 250 notes and community average 9.2 across 4090 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Squash & Root Vegetables, Meaty & Oily Fish, Shellfish, Crab & Lobster, Cake & Cream, Soft Cheese, Hard Cheese, Salads & Greens, Salami & Prosciutto.

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Veuve Clicquot 1993 Champagne

🍾 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$$ premium
Research bucket
Collector / benchmark bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack finds the 1993 Veuve more butterscotch-like and reduced, with cooked apples, cooked pears, nutmeg, white flowers, caramel apple, and old-white-wine character. The tiny bubbles create a very different experience from younger Champagne, and he says it needs to be drunk quickly.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 9.0 across 250 notes and community average 9.2 across 4090 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Squash & Root Vegetables, Meaty & Oily Fish, Shellfish, Crab & Lobster, Cake & Cream, Soft Cheese, Hard Cheese, Salads & Greens, Salami & Prosciutto.

$ Rank

Research bucket
Collector / benchmark bottles

Mack's Thoughts

Mack finds the non-vintage Veuve Clicquot familiar, with marzipan, wet-rock minerality, lemon, lilacs, toastiness, pear, acid, medium-to-full body, and bubbles that re-fizz in the mouth. He calls it standard, basic entry-level Veuve and a great starting point.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Via de la Plata Cava, Spain

🍾 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack frames Via de la Plata as Spain's answer to Champagne and points to the Cava seal as a quality guarantee. He sees pale straw color and finds straw, hay, apple, quince, and a little oxidative caramel-apple maturity; for the price, he calls it amazing and a strong Champagne alternative at a fraction of the cost.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 8.5 across 2 notes and community average 8.7 across 23 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Duck, Shellfish, Goose, Mushrooms, Manchego & Parmesan, Game, Pork, Squash & Root Vegetables.

Mack's Thoughts

Mack places this in Italy and reads the Montepulciano d'Abruzzo as plum, blackberry, leather, high tannin, and bright acid. He sees enough structure for beef stew or steak and says it has longevity for an under-$15 bottle.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Vina Borgia Garnacha, Spain

🍷 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack identifies Vina Borgia as Spanish Garnacha and finds that it looks and smells like wine rather than chemicals, with a little sulfur and hot oil. The alcohol reads hot at 14.5%, but the wine is dry, correctly structured, and in line with Spain's strong under-$15 value reputation, earning a big thumbs up.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 8.7 across 10 notes and community average 8.6 across 198 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Paella.

Other / blend · Video tasting

Vinchingo The Violet Swell Carignan, Redwood Valley / California

🍷 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack's captured note is direct: "Mack reads the clear bottle and no-capsule presentation as natural-wine signals and goes in with high hopes. He finds cherry, mint, wood spice, freshness, and sound natural-wine character without going off the deep end, calling it a cool B-plus wine.". The tone is quick, personal, and more about the moment of the bottle than a formal grid of fruit, acid, and tannin. There is no separate score attached here, so the note has to carry the whole read.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

No separate sommelier/critic note has been verified into this card yet, so no source-specific outside-note links are shown.

Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack finds King Family Roseland aromatic, honeydew-like, juicy, flavorful, Juicy Fruit-like, textured, dry, and not sweet. He says the blend of Viognier aromatics, Petit Manseng acid, and Chardonnay body works, and calls Virginia one of the most impressive and exciting wine regions in the tasting.

🌹 🔎 🛒?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$ value
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack places Von Winning in Germany's Pfalz and explains Pinot Noir as Spatburgunder. He gets strawberry, lemon custard, ripe roundness, no residual sugar, and a comforting petrol-like mechanical-shop note, calling it a good and fun example of a German rose category you do not often see.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Mack's Thoughts

Mack uses Von Winning Sekt to explain Germany's everyday sparkling-wine culture and Riesling-based Sekt. He sees pale yellow color and gets brioche, caramel apple, confectionary sweetness, and classic Riesling petrol, calling the wine fascinating while noting that import costs likely make it feel expensive for American buyers.

VRAC Rose 2020, Mediterranean France

🌹 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$ budget
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack sees VRAC Rose as pale and properly rose-colored, but the nose reads a little fishy with raspberry. The wine is dry, alcohol-heavy, light on fruit, and uncertain because the broad 12-to-14% alcohol range seems to land toward the heavier side, leaving him with a thinking-emoji reaction.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Chenin Blanc · Video tasting

Wade Cellars Chenin Blanc / California White Wine 2019, Dwyane Wade

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack treats Three by Wade as a 2019 California white wine built mostly from Chenin Blanc, a Loire grape popular with wine people. He gets honeydew melon, honeysuckle, jelly-bean fruit, possible sweetness on the nose, great acid, ripe honeydew, and chamomile. He calls it a great American Chenin Blanc expression, praises the Jason Pahlmeyer partnership, names it his overall favorite, and says it is balling.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

No separate sommelier/critic note has been verified into this card yet, so no source-specific outside-note links are shown.

Mack points out Wineberry's South of France origin and listed blend of Grenache, Carignan, and Syrah. He gets dark color, spice, nutmeg, cinnamon, heavy almost syrupy texture, bitter chocolate, and a mousy funk he likes, calling it a step up from traditional boxed wine even though it tastes more New World than place-specific Cotes-du-Rhone.

Wolffer Estate Summer in a Bottle Rose, Long Island
Region
Long Island, New York
Varietal / style
Rose
Mack rating
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
Mack note
Mack starts with Wolffer Estate Summer in a Bottle from Long Island and sees a pale salmon color. He gets cotton candy, stone fruit, peaches, apricots, wet sea stone, a yellow-Skittle citrus hint, grapefruit on the palate, and calls it refreshing, electric, tasty, and spot-on for $19.99.
Taster
André Hueston Mack
Community / pro ratings
Delectable pro rating: 8.7 (17); Delectable community rating: 8.8 (289).
Top notes / pairings
Potato, Sweet Potato, Yuca, Taro, Quinoa, Farro, Brown Rice, White Rice, Pasta, Exotic Spices, Beans & Peas, Onion, Shallot, Garlic

Champagne / sparkling · Video tasting

Wyoming: Jackson Hole Winery Old Faithful Sparkling Wine

🍾 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$ standard
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack says Jackson Hole Old Faithful smells like apple cider and dough, with fine bubbles and a more vintage-like feel. He would drink it, likes the low mousse because the Chardonnay essence comes through, and finds green apple, Asian pear, and elegance in the Wyoming-bottled Sonoma fruit.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

No separate sommelier/critic note has been verified into this card yet, so no source-specific outside-note links are shown.

Cabernet / Bordeaux · Video tasting

Yao Family Wines Napa Valley Cabernet Sauvignon 2018, Yao Ming

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$$$ premium
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack sees bright, clear, resilient color in Yao Family Napa Cabernet and gets cassis, raspberry, pencil shavings, plum, vanilla, oak expression, and real tannin structure. He calls it a good California Cabernet and quality wine, but at $98 he wants more detail on vineyard sourcing; he sees it as perfect for steak and grilling.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 8.4 across 16 notes and community average 8.9 across 124 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Onion, Shallot, Garlic, Pasta, Venison, Tomato-Based, Potato, Pork, Chocolate & Caramel, White Rice.

Yellow Tail Cabernet Sauvignon

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👎🏾 Thumbs down from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$ value
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack recognizes Yellow Tail's role in making Australian wine huge in the U.S., but the glass itself reads too polished to him. He finds anise, blackberry, boysenberry, bubblegum, sweetness, unnatural roundness, and a color that feels engineered.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit

Chardonnay · Video tasting

Yellow Tail Chardonnay 2019

🥂 🔎 ✨?
Rank
👍🏾 Thumbs up from Mack notes
$ Rank
$$ value
Research bucket
Episode bottles
Buy wine
Search Wine-Searcher
Mack's Thoughts

Mack notes that Yellow Tail Chardonnay is inexpensive and widely available. He finds lots and lots of vanilla, sweet-smelling peach, fruit-cocktail richness, ripe sweetness, and a soft-drink-like appeal, saying he can understand why people like it even though it reads like lollipops and cream soda.

Reviewed at Bon Appetit
What Others Say

Delectable verifies outside public context for this bottle with pro average 8.0 across 4 notes and community average 8.2 across 387 ratings. Delectable's pairing/context tags include Duck, Shellfish, Goose, Mushrooms, Manchego & Parmesan, Game, Pork, Squash & Root Vegetables.
